title: Russia Tensions Soar as Shelling, War Rhetoric Increase; Investors Flee Stocks
text: U.S. sees imminent Russian attack on Ukraine as artillery fire in the eastern part of the country picks up. Stocks are headed for another weekly decline.

title: US stocks expected to rally modestly at open on Friday after the Dow Jones suffered its steepest one-day loss of 2022
text: US stocks are expected to rally modestly at the open on Friday a day after the Dow Jones Industrial Average suffered its steepest one-day loss of 2022 as investors track the escalating violence in Ukraine and worries over a Russian invasion of the country. Futures for the blue-chip Dow Jones rose 0.4% after dropping more than 600 points, or 1.8% on Thursday.

title: 3 Dow Stocks To Avoid
text: Buying yield can be a healthy strategy, but when it comes to three Dow stocks dogged-looking price charts may quickly trump income generation. The post 3 Dow Stocks To Avoid appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: 5 Best Dow Dividend Stocks to Buy Now

title: Dow Jones Newswires: Ukraine invasion worries spark rush for Australian gold stocks
text: Investors are rushing to gold-mining stocks amid concerns that Russia could invade Ukraine, providing a windfall for a sector that had been softening on expectations of rising rates, which typically spell weakness for gold.

title: Dow Jones Newswires: Australia's Crown Resorts agrees to $6.3 billion takeover by Blackstone
text: SYDNEY — Australian casino operator Crown Resorts Ltd. said Monday it has agreed to a takeover proposal from U.S. investment firm Blackstone Inc. that values the company's equity at 8.9 billion Australian dollars (US$6.3 billion).

title: Kicking Off Super Bowl Weekend in the Green
text: After a precipitous drop Thursday of between -1.5% (Dow) and -2% (Nasdaq) we're seeing both the Dow and Nasdaq to +40 points, with the S&P 500 +7 points.

title: Dow Jones Newswires: Shell, BP amassed cash in 2021 as energy shortage unfolded
text: High oil and gas prices boosted forecast-beating 2021 profits for Shell and BP, and are expected to continue doing so this year, allowing both U.K. energy giants to strengthen their balance sheets for energy-transition investments.

title: Jerri DeVard nominated to Dow's Board of Directors
text: MIDLAND, Mich., Feb. 10, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Dow (NYSE: DOW) today announced its Board of Directors has nominated Jerri DeVard, former executive vice president and chief customer officer for Office Depot, Inc., to stand for election at the Company's Annual Meeting of Stockholders on April 14.

title: Newmark Global Corporate Services Renews Multi-Service Global Contract with Dow, Providing Client-Centric Integrated Solutions Across Six Continents
text: NEW YORK, Feb. 10, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Newmark Global Corporate Services (GCS) announces the renewal of a long-term, multi-service, international contract with Dow (NYSE:DOW), a Fortune 100 company and materials science leader, demonstrating Newmark's commitment to deliver superior solutions for corporate real estate clients. Under the terms of the contract, Newmark GCS professionals will support Dow's materials science operations housed in more than 150 buildings across 28 countries on six continents, including leased and owned offices space, Research & Development laboratories, warehouses, airport hangers, campus facilities and multi-purpose spaces.

title: Dow named a CDP Supplier Engagement Leader
text: MIDLAND, Mich., Feb. 10, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Dow (NYSE: DOW) has been recognized as a Global Supplier Engagement Leader by CDP, a global nonprofit that runs the global disclosure system for investors, companies, cities, states, and regions to manage their environmental impacts.

title: MarketWatch First Take: Google is finally splitting its stock; will Amazon be next, leading to a Dow shakeup?
text: Alphabet Inc. is finally carving into its gargantuan stock price with a stock split, which leaves only one Big Tech company remaining with a huge per-share price despite an easy avenue to change it.
